name: Laguna del Voluntário by the Puente Romano de Cagánchez — circular from Santa Amalia
name_original: Laguna del Voluntário por el Puente Romano de Cagánchez — circular desde Santa Amalia
region_intro: Hiking around Santa Amalia offers a diverse landscape characterized by the Guadiana River, its tributaries, and the nearby Cornalvo Natural Park. The region features a mix of open plains, reservoir areas, and historical elements like Roman bridges. Trails often follow riverbanks or lead through agricultural land, providing varied scenery. Best hiking trails around Santa Amalia The most popular hiking route is Cornalvo Reservoir Loop, a 4.7 miles (7.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 56 minutes to complete. This easy route circles the tranquil Cornalvo Reservoir. Another top favourite among local hikers is Laguna del Voluntário by the Puente Romano de Cagánchez — circular from Santa Amalia, a moderate 8.3 miles (13.3 km) path. This route explores the Laguna del Voluntário and passes by the historic Roman Bridge of Cagánchez. Local hikers also love the Ascent to the castle of Medellín — circular by the banks of the Río Ortiga, a 6.7 miles (10.8 km) trail leading through riverbanks and up to a historic castle, often completed in about 2 hours 47 minutes. Hiking around Santa Amalia is defined by river valleys, reservoir landscapes, and historical sites such as Roman bridges. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and challenging routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Santa Amalia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 100 hikers have used komoot to explore Santa Amalia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Santa Amalia?
A: There are over 20 hiking routes in the Santa Amalia region, offering a variety of experiences from easy strolls to more challenging treks. The komoot community has explored these trails over 100 times.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Santa Amalia?
A: Yes, Santa Amalia offers 10 easy hiking routes perfect for beginners or those seeking a relaxed walk. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1290567586/puente-romano-de-caganchez-y-rio-guadiana-circular-desde-medellin" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Romano de Cagánchez y Río Guadiana — circular desde Medellín&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 3.4-mile (5.5 km) path.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Santa Amalia?
A: The region is characterized by diverse landscapes, including the Guadiana River and its tributaries, open plains, and reservoir areas. You'll also encounter historical elements like Roman bridges and the natural beauty of the nearby Cornalvo Natural Park.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Santa Amalia?
A: Yes, many routes in Santa Amalia are circular. For example, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1291247472/presa-de-cornalvo-from-san-pedro-de-merida-circular-through-the-parque-natural-de-cornalvo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Presa de Cornalvo from San Pedro de Mérida — circular through the Parque Natural de Cornalvo&lt;/a&gt; offers a 7.6-mile (12.3 km) loop through the natural park.

Q: What are some notable attractions or landmarks I can see along the hiking trails?
A: Hikers can discover several interesting landmarks. You might encounter the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5339153" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medellín Roman Bridge&lt;/a&gt; or enjoy views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5725452" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Theater and Castle of Medellín&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3071953" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornalvo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2990429" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornalvo Dam&lt;/a&gt; are also prominent features in the area.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Santa Amalia suitable for families with children?
A: Many of the easy and moderate trails are suitable for families. The varied terrain, including riverbanks and reservoir paths, provides engaging scenery for all ages. Consider routes that are shorter and have less elevation gain for younger children.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Santa Amalia?
A: Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant hiking conditions in Santa Amalia, with milder temperatures. Summers can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable. Winters are typically mild, but some trails might be muddy after rain.

Q: Are there any hiking routes that offer opportunities for wildlife spotting?
A: The Cornalvo Natural Park, which borders the Santa Amalia region, is known for its biodiversity. Trails passing through or near the park, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1291247472/presa-de-cornalvo-from-san-pedro-de-merida-circular-through-the-parque-natural-de-cornalvo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Presa de Cornalvo from San Pedro de Mérida&lt;/a&gt;, offer good chances to observe local wildlife, especially around the reservoir and wooded areas.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Santa Amalia?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 20 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse scenery, the historical points of interest like Roman bridges, and the well-maintained paths that cater to various fitness levels.

Q: Is there public transport available to reach the trailheads around Santa Amalia?
A: While Santa Amalia itself is accessible by public transport, reaching specific trailheads outside the town often requires a car. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for connections to nearby villages if you plan to rely on public transport for your starting point.

Q: Are there any longer hiking options for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for more experienced hikers, there are moderate and difficult routes available. The region offers 10 moderate and 1 difficult trail. These longer routes often explore more extensive areas of the natural park or follow the Guadiana River for greater distances.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Santa Amalia?
A: Most natural areas and trails in Spain are dog-friendly, especially if your dog is kept on a leash. However, it's always recommended to check specific park regulations, particularly within the Cornalvo Natural Park, and ensure your dog is well-behaved and doesn't disturb wildlife or other hikers.
tips: Bridge located about 4 km. from Yelbes through Tentudia street.
